mnemonics.title: R.O.C.E.
mnemonics.content: Reusability, Organization, Collaboration, Efficiency - Four key benefits of using modules in IoT systems

mnemonics.title: C.I.T.U.
mnemonics.content: Create > Import > Test > Use - Module implementation sequence for IoT development

mnemonics.title: Main Guard
mnemonics.content: Remember __name__ == '__main__' as a security guard deciding if code runs directly (main entry) or gets imported

acronyms.expansion: Modular Organization Develops Usable Logical Elements
acronyms.usage: Core philosophy behind Python module system

acronyms.expansion: Including Modules Provides Organized Reusable Tools
acronyms.usage: Purpose of import statements in IoT systems
Remember where to find 300,000+ modules for IoT projects

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a toolbox - Each drawer (module) contains specific tools (functions) for sensor operations, data processing, etc.
Picture library bookshelves - Each shelf (module) keeps related books (functions) separated from others
Visualize a house door - This file turns directories into importable modules (packages)
Imagine a GPS navigation system checking locations in order: Current dir > PYTHONPATH > Standard lib > Site-packages

analogies.analogy: Like smartphone app compartments - Each module is a separate app handling specific tasks (Camera, Messages, Weather)
Similar to restaurant kitchen stations - Import specific stations (modules) like GrillStation, FryStation, SaladStation as needed
Like a city's infrastructure - Pre-built modules (os=roads, sys=power grid, json=post office) support IoT systems
